content: parsejson  Table of contents Prototype: parsejson(json_data) Return type: data Description: Parses JSON data directly from an inlined string and returns the result as a data variable Arguments: json_data : string - JSON string to parse - in the range: .* Please note that because JSON uses double quotes, it's usually most convenient to use single quotes for the string (CFEngine allows both types of quotes around a string). This function can accept many types of data parameters. Example: code vars : "loadthis" data => parsejson ( ' { "key" : "value" } ' ); # inline syntax since 3.7 "loadthis_inline" data => ' { "key" : "value" } ' ; Notes: This functions does not parse primitives . History: Introduced in CFEngine 3.6.0 The collecting function behavior was added in 3.9. See also: readjson() , parseyaml() , readyaml() , mergedata() , Inline YAML and JSON data , about collecting functions , and data documentation.
